title: Broken update to 3.3.1 on Ubuntu 20.04
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

On Ubuntu 20.04 update to podman 3.3.1 loses strict dependency on `containernetworking-plugins` package making installation incomplete.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Run standard package update procedure.

1. `sudo apt update`; result lists podman 3.3.1 as one of updates
2. `sudo apt upgrade`; podman 3.3.1 update is kept back because package deps changed and update must be done manually
3. `sudo apt install --reinstall podman`; `apt` suggests running `autoremove` because `containernetworking-plugins` is no longer dependency
4. `sudo apt autoremove` removes `containernetworking-plugins` package

**Describe the results you received:**

Somewhat broken podman installation, running `podman info --debug` results with warnings coming from missing networking plugins:

```
$ podman info --debug
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /home/jarek/.config/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman runs without errors or warnings

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Goes away after manually installing `containernetworking-plugins` package

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Not applicable

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: I had missed this one too, last night I only saw a notification for containers-common. Anyway, it's bein updated on OBS now. Will update once it lands.
should be fixed now. Please give it a try. Closing..

this is not working for me -- I am installing `podman-rootless` and seeing this error still:

```console
$ podman run --rm -ti ubuntu:focal echo hi
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /home/asottile/.config/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
WARN[0002] Error validating CNI config file /home/asottile/.config/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/libexec/cni /usr/lib/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
hi
```

Rootless Podman now requires the CNI plugins (we can still function to some degree without them, as seen here, but we are making steps to use them by default). The original intent of the `podman-rootless` package was a version of Podman that did not have a dependency on CNI (to avoid conflicts with Kubernetes-shipped CNI), which means you likely don't have it installed. @lsm5 Can we add CNI as a recommends to `podman-rootless`? In the meantime, you can just install CNI and it should begin to work.

I'm on podman 3.3.0 (so not just affecting 3.3.1), only recently installed (also 20.04), with the same messages, and so far I haven't noticed anything not working; it just became distracting with all the warnings output each time I ran any command. Thanks for the fix!!

Just ran into this - on 3.4.2 and ubuntu 20.04; So I suppose this is due to using Kubic package repo? Warnings go away after installing `containernetworking-plugins` (not that anything else wasn't working)
